notes: All 61 works resolved; none left at 0x0. All values are height x width of the painted/printed surface in cm, rounded to one decimal. Methodology: Met and AIC works from their public APIs (Met elementMeasurements in cm, preferring Overall/Painting/Sheet; AIC dimensions text); Commons works resolved File -> structured-data P6243 (digital representation of) -> Wikidata P2048/P2049, taking preferred-rank claims and converting units. Raw per-work JSON with API titles and dimension strings: /private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-staticzero-Developer-morning-masterpieces/e7bfb60c-0dd1-4ac2-9650-e25caa47bf45/scratchpad/dims-results.json (script dims.mjs alongside). Special cases: (1) Bosch — reported as full OPEN triptych; Wikidata Q321303 carries both the Prado's current official 205.5 x 384.9 (preferred rank, used here) and the older 220 x 390 that matches the task spec's ~220 x 389; pick either, the aspect ratio differs by <1%. (2) Michelangelo Creation of Adam and Raphael School of Athens are frescoes with no canonical panel measurement — task-spec approximations, low confidence. (3) Hokusai (25.7 x 37.9, horizontal oban) and Hiroshige (34 x 24.1, vertical oban) use the Met's single Overall print measurement; Sargent Green Door uses the watercolor sheet (53 x 35.9), not the mat. (4) Turner venice-salute-porch is a commons seed but the underlying object is Met 437853, so the Met API was used. (5) Manet Blue Venice (Shelburne) is the only medium-confidence entry: Wikidata and the Wikipedia Manet catalogue agree on 54 x 65 but the museum's own page could not be checked. Minor sub-cm variances exist between Wikidata and museum pages for a few works (Gleaners 83 vs 83.5 height, Whistler's Mother 163 vs 162.4 width, Young Hare 25 vs 25.1) — immaterial for aspect-ratio purposes. Sanity checks pass: Vermeers ~45 cm, Leutze largest at 378.5 x 647.7, portrait-orientation works (Madame X, Cassatt, Hiroshige, Munch, Friedrich, El Greco) have height > width as expected.
